chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The (saying of) ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’ in the oaths

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/328)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/328)
text: لِلْعَبْدِ أَنْ يَسْتَثْنِيَ فِي الْيَمِينِ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْماً إِذَا نَسِيَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Husayn Al Qalanasy, or one of his companioins,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said to the worshipper: ‘The exclusion in the oath is in what is between it and forty days, when you forget’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Husayn al- Qalanisiy or certain persons of his people who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A servant (of Allah) can make the exception up to forty days if he forgets.’”
